source.note: Unit 3 Grammar 1 (Contrast with but and instead, planner pp.106-109). Student Book pp.44-45, Workbook answer key p.29, and Grammar Workbook answer key pp.10-11 were also checked for source-backed samples and exercises.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Contrast with but and instead
grammarPoints.rule: Use but to join two contrasting ideas in one sentence. Use instead at the end of a second sentence to show a different action or idea.
grammarPoints.pattern: past idea + , but + present idea  |  past idea. different idea + instead

chart.title: Contrast with but and instead

intro_examples.text: Long ago, children walked to school, but today many children ride their bikes.
intro_examples.jp: 昔、子どもたちは学校まで歩いて行きましたが、今では多くの子どもたちは自転車に乗ります。

intro_examples.text: Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories instead.
intro_examples.jp: 何年も前、人々にはテレビがありませんでした。そのかわりに物語を語りました。

rows.example: Long ago, children walked to school, but today many children ride their bikes.
rows.jp: but は2つの対照的な考えを1つの文につなげます。

rows.example: Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories instead.
rows.jp: instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置き、「そのかわりに」という意味を表します。

rows.example: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces, too.
rows.jp: too は違いではなく、同じことを表すときに使います。

chart.note_rule: Use but for contrast inside one sentence. Use instead at the end of the second sentence.
chart.note_exception: Use a comma before but. Use a period before the second sentence with instead.
chart.note_exception_detail: Use too when things are the same: Today some people have fireplaces, too.

table.title: Contrasting long ago and today

cells: Long ago, children walked to school
today many children ride their bikes
Many years ago, people didn't have TV.
Today some people have fireplaces, too.

notes: Use but when two ideas are different.
Use instead at the end of the second sentence.
Use too when two ideas are the same.

tab1_samples.text: Long ago, children walked to school, but today many children ride their bikes.
tab1_samples.jp: 昔、子どもたちは学校まで歩いて行きましたが、今では多くの子どもたちは自転車に乗ります。

tab1_samples.text: Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories instead.
tab1_samples.jp: 何年も前、人々にはテレビがありませんでした。そのかわりに物語を語りました。

tab1_samples.text: Long ago, mothers and daughters sewed clothes at home. Today we buy clothes in stores instead.
tab1_samples.jp: 昔、母親と娘たちは家で服を縫いました。今ではそのかわりに店で服を買います。

tab1_samples.text: Many years ago, people couldn't play video games, but they played some fun board games.
tab1_samples.jp: 何年も前、人々はビデオゲームをすることはできませんでしたが、楽しいボードゲームをしました。

tab1_samples.text: Long ago, people didn't have computers. They wrote letters instead.
tab1_samples.jp: 昔、人々にはコンピューターがありませんでした。そのかわりに手紙を書きました。

tab1_samples.text: Many years ago, people didn't have cars, but some people had horses.
tab1_samples.jp: 何年も前、人々には車がありませんでしたが、馬を持っている人もいました。

tab1_samples.text: Years ago, we didn't have cell phones, but we had a big red telephone.
tab1_samples.jp: 何年も前、私たちには携帯電話はありませんでしたが、大きな赤い電話がありました。
私たちはそのかわりにおもちゃで遊びました。

tab1_samples.text: I don't have any dolls, but I have a lot of puppets.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は人形を持っていませんが、たくさんの操り人形を持っています。

tab1_samples.text: My grandmother washed clothes by hand, but I use a washing machine instead!
tab1_samples.jp: 私の祖母は手で服を洗いましたが、私はそのかわりに洗濯機を使います。

rules.title: Use but in the middle
rules.jp_title: but は文の途中で使う
rules.subtitle: But connects two complete contrasting ideas. In writing, put a comma before but.
rules.jp_subtitle: but は対照的な2つの考えをつなぎます。書くときは but の前にコンマを置きます。

transforms.from: Long ago, children walked to school. Today many children ride their bikes.
transforms.to: Long ago, children walked to school, but today many children ride their bikes.

transforms.from: Many years ago, people didn't have cars. Some people had horses.
transforms.to: Many years ago, people didn't have cars, but some people had horses.

examples.text: Then people used fires for light, but now we use electric lights.
examples.jp: そのころ人々は明かりに火を使いましたが、今では電灯を使います。

examples.text: Long ago, people made their own soap, but today we can buy soap at the store.
examples.jp: 昔、人々は自分で石けんを作りましたが、今では店で石けんを買えます。

rules.title: Use instead at the end
rules.jp_title: instead は文の終わりで使う
rules.subtitle: Instead shows a different action or idea in the second sentence.
rules.jp_subtitle: instead は2つ目の文で、別の行動や考えを表します。

transforms.from: Long ago, people made their own clothes, but today people buy them.
transforms.to: Long ago, people made their own clothes. Today people buy them instead.

transforms.from: In 1900, people took boats to other countries. Now they take planes instead.
transforms.to: In 1900, people took boats to other countries, but now they take planes.

examples.text: In the past, we walked to school. Today we ride a bus instead.
examples.jp: 昔、私たちは学校まで歩きました。今ではそのかわりにバスに乗ります。

examples.text: People used candles for light. Today we use electric lights instead.
examples.jp: 人々は明かりにろうそくを使いました。今ではそのかわりに電灯を使います。

rules.title: Time words show the contrast
rules.jp_title: 時を表す言葉が対比を助ける
rules.subtitle: Long ago, many years ago, and in the past point to the past. Today and now point to the present.
rules.jp_subtitle: Long ago / many years ago / in the past は過去を表し、today / now は現在を表します。

transforms.from: People had horses and carts. We have cars.
transforms.to: Long ago people had horses and carts. Today we have cars instead.

transforms.from: People wrote letters. Friends send e-mails.
transforms.to: Many years ago, friends wrote letters on paper. Now friends send e-mails instead.

examples.text: Long ago people had horses and carts. Today we have cars instead.
examples.jp: 昔、人々には馬と荷車がありました。今ではそのかわりに車があります。

examples.text: Many years ago, friends wrote letters on paper. Now friends send e-mails instead.
examples.jp: 何年も前、友だちは紙に手紙を書きました。今ではそのかわりにメールを送ります。

rules.title: Use too for things that are the same
rules.jp_title: 同じことには too を使う
rules.subtitle: Too does not show contrast. It shows that the second idea is also true.
rules.jp_subtitle: too は対比ではなく、2つ目のことも同じように正しいことを表します。

transforms.from: People had fireplaces long ago. Some people have fireplaces today.
transforms.to: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces, too.

transforms.from: People played board games long ago. Many people play board games today.
transforms.to: Long ago, people played board games. Many people play board games today, too.

examples.text: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces, too.
examples.jp: 昔、人々には暖炉がありました。今でも暖炉がある人もいます。

examples.text: Some people made their own soap long ago. Some people make their own soap today, too.
examples.jp: 昔、自分で石けんを作った人もいました。今でも自分で石けんを作る人もいます。

mixed_samples.text: Then people used fires for light, but now we use electric lights.
mixed_samples.jp: そのころ人々は明かりに火を使いましたが、今では電灯を使います。

mixed_samples.text: In the past, we walked to school. Today we ride a bus instead.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、私たちは学校まで歩きました。今ではそのかわりにバスに乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: Long ago, people made their own soap, but today we can buy soap at the store.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、人々は自分で石けんを作りましたが、今では店で石けんを買えます。

mixed_samples.text: People used candles for light. Today we use electric lights instead.
mixed_samples.jp: 人々は明かりにろうそくを使いました。今ではそのかわりに電灯を使います。

mixed_samples.text: Long ago people had horses and carts. Today we have cars instead.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、人々には馬と荷車がありました。今ではそのかわりに車があります。

mixed_samples.text: Many years ago, women wore long dresses, but today many women wear shorter dresses and pants.
mixed_samples.jp: 何年も前、女性は長いドレスを着ていましたが、今では短いドレスやズボンを着る女性が多いです。

mixed_samples.text: Long ago, many young children worked. Today they go to school instead.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、多くの幼い子どもたちは働いていました。今ではそのかわりに学校へ行きます。

mixed_samples.text: Long ago, families had many children, but now families are smaller.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、家族にはたくさんの子どもがいましたが、今では家族は小さくなっています。

mixed_samples.text: Many years ago, we grew our own food. Now we buy food from supermarkets instead.
mixed_samples.jp: 何年も前、私たちは自分たちで食べ物を育てました。今ではそのかわりにスーパーで食べ物を買います。

mixed_samples.text: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces, too.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、人々には暖炉がありました。今でも暖炉がある人もいます。

stem: Long ago, children walked to school,
today many children ride their bikes.

explanation.title: Use but
explanation.body: But joins two contrasting ideas in one sentence.

tab3_quiz.jp: but は対照的な2つの考えを1つの文につなげます。

stem: Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories

explanation.title: Use instead
explanation.body: Instead comes at the end of the second sentence.

tab3_quiz.jp: instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置きます。

stem: Long ago, people didn't have computers. They wrote letters

explanation.title: End with instead
explanation.body: The second sentence shows a different action.

tab3_quiz.jp: 2つ目の文が別の行動を表すので、instead を使います。

stem: Many years ago, people couldn't play video games,
they played some fun board games.

explanation.title: Middle connector
explanation.body: Use but in the middle to connect the contrast.

tab3_quiz.jp: 1つの文の途中で対比をつなぐときは but を使います。

stem: Which sentence uses instead correctly?
answers: Long ago, people walked, instead today we ride.
Long ago, people walked. Today we ride bikes instead.
Long ago, people walked instead, but today.

explanation.title: Instead goes at the end
explanation.body: Instead belongs at the end of the second sentence.

tab3_quiz.jp: instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置くのが自然です。

stem: Use a comma before
when it connects two contrasting ideas.

explanation.title: Comma before but
explanation.body: In writing, a comma is usually used before but.

tab3_quiz.jp: 書くときは、ふつう but の前にコンマを置きます。

stem: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces,

explanation.title: Same idea
explanation.body: Too shows that the same thing is true now.

tab3_quiz.jp: 同じことが今も当てはまるときは too を使います。

stem: Years ago, we didn't have cell phones,
we had a big red telephone.

explanation.title: Contrast in one sentence
explanation.body: Use but to contrast the two phone ideas.

tab3_quiz.jp: 2つの電話についての考えを対比しているので but を使います。

stem: Long ago, many young children worked. Today they go to school

explanation.title: Different action
explanation.body: Going to school replaces working, so use instead.

tab3_quiz.jp: 働くことのかわりに学校へ行くので instead を使います。

stem: Many years ago, women wore long dresses,
today many women wear shorter dresses and pants.

explanation.title: Use but for contrast
explanation.body: The sentence contrasts clothing long ago and today.

tab3_quiz.jp: 昔と今の服装を対比しているので but を使います。

tab4_master.jp: 昔、子どもたちは学校まで歩いて行きましたが、今では多くの子どもたちは自転車に乗ります。

stem: Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories

explanation.title: Use instead
explanation.body: Instead belongs at the end of the second sentence.

tab4_master.jp: instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置きます。

tab4_master.cue: Build the second sentence with instead.
tab4_master.jp: 彼らはそのかわりに手紙を書きました。

stem: Which punctuation is usually used before but?

explanation.title: Comma before but
explanation.body: Use a comma before but when it joins two complete contrasting ideas.

tab4_master.jp: 対照的な2つの考えを but でつなぐときは、ふつう but の前にコンマを置きます。
何年も前、人々には車がありませんでしたが、馬を持っている人もいました。

stem: Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces,

explanation.title: Use too
explanation.body: Too shows that the same thing is true in both times.

tab4_master.jp: 同じことが昔も今も当てはまるときは too を使います。
今ではそのかわりに店で服を買います。

stem: Choose the correct sentence.
answers: Long ago people had horses and carts. Today we have cars instead.
Long ago people had horses and carts instead. Today we have cars.
Long ago people had horses and carts, instead today we have cars.
Instead long ago people had horses and carts, today we have cars.

explanation.title: Correct instead position
explanation.body: The word instead should come at the end of the second sentence.

tab4_master.jp: instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置きます。
昔、家族にはたくさんの子どもがいましたが、今では家族は小さくなっています。

stem: When should we use too?
answers: when the two ideas are the same
when the second idea replaces the first

explanation.title: Same, not contrast
explanation.body: Too shows the second idea is also true.

tab4_master.jp: too は2つ目のことも同じように正しいときに使います。

japanese.title: but と instead を使った対比
japanese.rule: but は1つの文の中で対照的な2つの考えをつなぎます。instead は2つ目の文の終わりに置き、「そのかわりに」という意味を表します。
japanese.pattern: 過去の考え + , but + 今の考え / 過去の考え。今の別の考え + instead

examples.sentence: Long ago, children walked to school, but today many children ride their bikes.
Many years ago, people didn't have TV. They told stories instead.
Long ago, mothers and daughters sewed clothes at home. Today we buy clothes in stores instead.
Many years ago, people couldn't play video games, but they played some fun board games.
Long ago, people didn't have computers. They wrote letters instead.
Many years ago, people didn't have cars, but some people had horses.
Years ago, we didn't have cell phones, but we had a big red telephone.
We played with toys instead.
I don't have any dolls, but I have a lot of puppets.
My grandmother washed clothes by hand, but I use a washing machine instead!
Then people used fires for light, but now we use electric lights.
In the past, we walked to school. Today we ride a bus instead.
Long ago, people made their own soap, but today we can buy soap at the store.
People used candles for light. Today we use electric lights instead.
Long ago people had horses and carts. Today we have cars instead.
Many years ago, women wore long dresses, but today many women wear shorter dresses and pants.
Long ago, many young children worked. Today they go to school instead.
Long ago, families had many children, but now families are smaller.
Many years ago, we grew our own food. Now we buy food from supermarkets instead.
Long ago, people had fireplaces. Today some people have fireplaces, too.

workbookActivities.instruction: Read. Complete the sentences with but or instead.
Read and write. Rewrite the sentences without the underlined word. Use but or instead.
Look and write. Finish the sentences using but or instead.

source.note: Chart, samples, and exercises are sourced from the planner grammar box, Be the Expert notes, Student Book, Workbook answer key, and Grammar Workbook answer key.
